channel.title: Beginner on Built by Nikhil
channel.description: Recent content in Beginner on Built by Nikhil

item.title: Day 1: Intro to Arduino & Your First Code
item.description: Day 1 of Learn Coding with Arduino — meet your Uno, install the IDE, and make the little built-in LED blink.

item.title: Day 2: Blinking an External LED & Understanding the Breadboard
item.description: Day 2 of Learn Coding with Arduino — wire up your own LED on a breadboard, learn why resistors matter, and blink it from the Uno.

item.title: Day 3: Button Input — Making the Arduino Listen
item.description: Day 3 of Learn Coding with Arduino — connect a push button, read it with digitalRead, and use your first if/else to turn on an LED only when pressed.

item.title: Day 4: Variables and Loops
item.description: Day 4 of Learn Coding with Arduino — meet variables (labeled boxes that hold numbers) and for loops (repeat without retyping). Blink an LED 5 times in a row with just a few lines.

item.title: Day 5: Three LEDs — Dance, Chase, and Random
item.description: Day 5 of Learn Coding with Arduino — wire up 3 LEDs and control them as a group. Chaser patterns, random blinks, and the trick of looping over pin numbers.

item.title: Day 6: Build a Traffic Light System
item.description: Day 6 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a full red-yellow-green traffic light with realistic timing, then add a pedestrian button that overrides the cycle.

item.title: Day 7: The Easier Button — INPUT_PULLUP
item.description: Day 7 of Learn Coding with Arduino — simplify your button circuit using the Arduino’s built-in pull-up. One resistor less, and learn how to toggle an LED with each press.

item.title: Day 8: Potentiometer, Serial Monitor, and Dimming an LED
item.description: Day 8 of Learn Coding with Arduino — your first analog input. Read a potentiometer knob, print numbers to the Serial Monitor, and use PWM to dim an LED smoothly.

item.title: Day 9: Light Sensor — Arduino Learns to See
item.description: Day 9 of Learn Coding with Arduino — wire up a photoresistor (LDR) to read how bright the room is, and use a threshold to decide if it’s dark enough to turn on a light.

item.title: Day 10: Project — Smart Night Light
item.description: Day 10 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a smart night light that turns on automatically when it gets dark, with a manual override button.

item.title: Day 11: Buzzer — Make Noise and a Simple Melody
item.description: Day 11 of Learn Coding with Arduino — connect a piezo buzzer and use tone() to play beeps, notes, and a short melody.

item.title: Day 12: Servo Motor — Rotate to Any Angle
item.description: Day 12 of Learn Coding with Arduino — hook up an SG90 servo motor, sweep it from 0° to 180°, and control its position with a potentiometer.

item.title: Day 13: DC Motor, Transistor, and Speed Control
item.description: Day 13 of Learn Coding with Arduino — drive a DC motor with a transistor and control its speed using PWM. Meet the diode and understand why Arduino can’t power motors directly.

item.title: Day 14: Project — Auto Fan System
item.description: Day 14 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build an automatic fan that turns on when the room gets too bright or too hot, with a buzzer alert. Everything from Days 1-13 in one project.

item.title: Day 15: LCD Screen — Hello Anish!
item.description: Day 15 of Learn Coding with Arduino — hook up a 16×2 LCD via I2C and display your first text. Arduino gets a screen!

item.title: Day 16: Show Sensor Values on the LCD
item.description: Day 16 of Learn Coding with Arduino — display live potentiometer and LDR readings on the 16x2 LCD without flicker. Arduino gets a dashboard.

item.title: Day 17: Build an LCD Menu You Can Navigate
item.description: Day 17 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a scrollable menu on the 16x2 LCD. Two buttons: one to cycle through options, one to select. Arduino gets a real user interface.

item.title: Day 18: Project — Mini Weather Station
item.description: Day 18 of Learn Coding with Arduino — build a mini weather station that shows light level, simulated temperature, and custom sun and moon icons on the LCD.

item.title: Day 19: IR Remote — Control Arduino with a TV Remote
item.description: Day 19 of Learn Coding with Arduino — hook up an IR receiver and use any TV or DVD remote to control LEDs. Your first wireless input.

item.title: Day 20: Sound Sensor — Clap to Control
item.description: Day 20 of Learn Coding with Arduino — wire up a KY-038 sound sensor, detect claps, and toggle an LED with a sound.

item.title: Day 21: Dual-Mode Control — IR Remote + Sound
item.description: Day 21 of Learn Coding with Arduino — combine the IR remote and sound sensor into one sketch with manual and auto modes, with the LCD showing the current state.

item.title: Day 22: Project — Smart Room System
item.description: Day 22 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the big one. Build a Smart Room System that combines IR, sound, LCD, light sensor, LED, and buzzer into one coherent program.

item.title: Day 23: Pick Your Project — Brainstorm and Plan
item.description: Day 23 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the first day of your final project. Brainstorm ideas, pick one that excites you, and sketch the system diagram before writing any code.

item.title: Day 24: Build Your Project — One Piece at a Time
item.description: Day 24 of Learn Coding with Arduino — take the project plan from Day 23 and start building. One module at a time, tested and working, before moving to the next.

item.title: Day 25: Integrate and Debug
item.description: Day 25 of Learn Coding with Arduino — stitch all the modules from Day 24 into one coherent sketch, break loop() into helper functions, and debug integration issues.

item.title: Day 26: Demo Day — Show It Off
item.description: Day 26 of Learn Coding with Arduino — the final day. You present your project, celebrate the journey, and think about what to build next.
